expectations.evidence: File 'packages-nextjs-middleware.ts' exists in outputs directory with full x402 middleware implementation (60 lines, imports, paymentProxy config, matcher export).

expectations.text: Uses x402 v2 API (paymentProxy, x402ResourceServer, HTTPFacilitatorClient)
expectations.evidence: middleware.ts line 1: import { paymentProxy } from '@x402/next'; line 2: import { HTTPFacilitatorClient, x402ResourceServer } from '@x402/core/server'; line 12-13: const facilitatorClient = new HTTPFacilitatorClient({ url: facilitatorUrl }); const server = new x402ResourceServer(facilitatorClient);
middleware.ts line 3: import { registerExactEvmScheme } from '@x402/evm/exact/server'; line 14: registerExactEvmScheme(server);

expectations.text: Uses CAIP-2 network format (eip155:84532) not legacy names
expectations.evidence: .env.development line 11: NETWORK=eip155:84532 with comment '# CAIP-2 network identifier (eip155:84532 = Base Sepolia, eip155:8453 = Base Mainnet)'. middleware.ts line 9: const network = process.env.NETWORK as `${string}:${string}`; used in route config objects.

middleware.ts line 4-5: import { createPaywall } from '@x402/paywall'; import { evmPaywall } from '@x402/paywall/evm'; lines 17-24: const paywall = createPaywall().withNetwork(evmPaywall).withConfig({...}).build();

expectations.text: A protected API route handler exists
expectations.evidence: File 'packages-nextjs-app-api-payment-builder-route.ts' exists with an exported GET handler that returns a builder leaderboard JSON response. It is protected by the middleware matcher '/api/payment/:path*'.

expectations.text: Environment variables for facilitator, wallet, network configured
expectations.evidence: .env.development contains all three: NEXT_PUBLIC_FACILITATOR_URL=https://x402.org/facilitator, RESOURCE_WALLET_ADDRESS=0xYourAddressHere, NETWORK=eip155:84532. middleware.ts reads them via process.env.

expectations.text: x402 packages added to nextjs package.json
expectations.evidence: packages-nextjs-package.json lines 41-44: '@x402/core': '^2.2.0', '@x402/evm': '^2.2.0', '@x402/next': '^2.2.0', '@x402/paywall': '^2.2.0' all listed in dependencies.
middleware.ts lines 58-60: export const config = { matcher: ['/api/payment/:path*', '/payment/:path*'] }; This covers both API routes and page routes.
scaffold.config.ts line 16: targetNetworks: [chains.baseSepolia], with pollingInterval set to 3000 (appropriate for L2).

suggestions: Implementation is comprehensive and follows the x402 v2 API correctly across all files.
Good inclusion of a CLI testing script (send402request) in the hardhat package for programmatic verification.
The paywall configuration includes testnet:true which is appropriate for Base Sepolia.
Consider adding a .env.example in addition to .env.development for production deployment guidance.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score. All 10 expectations are met with clear, correct implementations. The middleware uses the full x402 v2 API surface (paymentProxy, HTTPFacilitatorClient, x402ResourceServer, registerExactEvmScheme, createPaywall with evmPaywall). CAIP-2 network format is used throughout. The protected API route, environment configuration, package dependencies, middleware matcher, and scaffold.config.ts are all correctly set up.
